MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
CARLOS JATO, Esq., 415.771.6174, cgjato@jato-law.com, for Plaintiff PATRICIA HERNANDEZ.
KELSEY J. MOE,, Esq., LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP 415.362.2580 Kelsey.Moe@lewisbrisbois.com, for Defendans TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT and AMBASSADOR SRO ASSOCIATES, LP

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMBASSADOR SRO ASSOCIATES, LP, TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT

"Defendants'" "motion to strike punitive damages from portions of plaintiff's first amended complaint" (FAC) is denied.

Plaintiff, a 72-year-old night clerk at defendants' SRO hotel, pleads that she was accosted by a guest the night of February 5, 2021. (FAC 3:8-18.) This culminated in the guest threatening to rape plaintiff, throwing a bottle at her and slapping her. (Id. at 3:16-18.) "Defendants' knew or should have known" the guest "was a violent individual with a criminal background" yet placed him in the hotel without providing physical barriers or security to protect night clerks such as her, plaintiff pleads. (Id. at 2:24-3:7.) These specific facts suffice to support a prayer for punitive damages at the pleading stage. (See Grieves v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 166-67.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)
